actors.description: Mac-Gray Corporation is the target company and described as the second largest laundry facilities management contractor in the United States.
actors.exact_quote: Mac-Gray Corporation is the second largest laundry facilities management contractor in the United States.
actors.label: Mac-Gray Corporation

actors.description: CSC ServiceWorks, Inc. is the buyer-side operating company and leading provider of multi-family housing and commercial laundry solutions.
actors.exact_quote: CSC ServiceWorks, Inc., with over one million machines in service, is the leading provider of multi-family housing and commercial laundry solutions as well as the industry leader in air vending services at convenience stores and gas stations.
actors.label: CSC ServiceWorks, Inc.

actors.description: CSC ServiceWorks Holdings, Inc. is the sole stockholder of CSC.
actors.exact_quote: CSC ServiceWorks Holdings, Inc. is a Delaware corporation and the sole stockholder of CSC.
actors.label: CSC ServiceWorks Holdings, Inc.

actors.description: Spin Holdco Inc. is a wholly-owned subsidiary of CSC.
actors.exact_quote: Spin Holdco Inc. is a Delaware corporation and a wholly-owned subsidiary of CSC.
actors.label: Spin Holdco Inc.

actors.description: CSC Fenway, Inc. is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Parent.
actors.exact_quote: CSC Fenway, Inc. is a Delaware corporation and a wholly-owned subsidiary of Parent.
actors.label: CSC Fenway, Inc.

actors.description: Pamplona acquired CSC in May 2013 after CSC had engaged in a sale process.
actors.exact_quote: During the fall and winter of 2012, CSC engaged in a sale process and, ultimately, CSC was purchased by Pamplona in May 2013.
actors.label: Pamplona

actors.description: Party A was an anonymous strategic party in the laundry facilities management industry.
actors.exact_quote: including opportunities with another strategic party, whom we refer to as Party A, in the laundry facilities management industry.
actors.label: Party A

actors.description: Party B was an anonymous financial bidder that entered the process and submitted proposals.
actors.exact_quote: 18 financial bidders (including Party B and Party C)
actors.label: Party B

actors.description: Party C was an anonymous financial bidder that entered the process and submitted proposals but later did not submit a final revised indication.
actors.exact_quote: 18 financial bidders (including Party B and Party C)
actors.label: Party C

actors.description: Moab Partners, L.P. and affiliates had nominated directors elected to Mac-Gray's Board after a proxy contest.
actors.exact_quote: Mac-Gray and Moab engaged in a proxy contest leading up to the annual meeting and Mr. Rothenberg and Mr. Hyman were elected in place of Mac-Gray's incumbent nominees, David W. Bryan and Mary Ann Tocio.
actors.label: Moab

actors.description: Moab beneficially owned approximately 9% of Mac-Gray common stock at the time Moab rollover issues were discussed.
actors.exact_quote: At the time of this meeting, Moab beneficially owned approximately 9% of Mac-Gray's outstanding common stock.
actors.label: Moab

actors.description: BofA Merrill Lynch was approved as Mac-Gray's financial advisor for strategic alternatives, including a potential sale process.
actors.exact_quote: Based on the recommendation of the Transaction Committee, the Board unanimously approved the engagement of BofA Merrill Lynch on the terms discussed at this meeting.
actors.label: BofA Merrill Lynch

actors.description: Goodwin Procter LLP served as outside legal counsel to Mac-Gray.
actors.exact_quote: representatives from BofA Merrill Lynch and Goodwin Procter LLP, outside legal counsel to Mac-Gray, were also in attendance
actors.label: Goodwin Procter LLP

actors.description: Kirkland & Ellis LLP served as outside legal counsel to CSC/Pamplona.
actors.exact_quote: Goodwin Procter delivered to Kirkland & Ellis LLP, outside legal counsel to CSC/Pamplona, whom we refer to as Kirkland, the initial draft of the merger agreement.
actors.label: Kirkland & Ellis LLP

actors.description: The Special Committee had six independent and disinterested directors: Bullock, Daoust, Hyman, Meagher, Percelay and Robinson.
actors.exact_quote: the Board then established the Special Committee, comprised of the six independent and disinterested members of the Board (namely, Mr. Bullock, Mr. Daoust, Mr. Hyman, William F. Meagher, Bruce A. Percelay and Alastair G. Robinson)
actors.label: Special Committee

actors.description: The Transaction Committee was formed with Thomas Bullock and Paul Daoust as members to assist the strategic alternatives review.
actors.exact_quote: the Board formed a transaction committee at this meeting, which we refer to as the Transaction Committee, and appointed Thomas Bullock and Paul Daoust, both independent directors, to serve as its members.
actors.label: Transaction Committee

bids.description: Party A submitted an unsolicited all-cash proposal to buy Mac-Gray for $17.00 to $19.00 per share.
bids.exact_quote: On June 21, 2013, Party A submitted an unsolicited proposal to the Mac-Gray Board of Directors offering to purchase Mac-Gray for an all-cash purchase price of $17.00 to $19.00 per share
bids.label: Party A June 21 proposal

bids.description: CSC/Pamplona submitted a preliminary all-cash indication of interest at $18.50 per share.
bids.exact_quote: On July 23, 2013, CSC/Pamplona submitted a preliminary indication of interest at an all-cash purchase price of $18.50 per share.
bids.label: CSC/Pamplona preliminary bid

bids.description: Party B submitted a preliminary all-cash indication at $17.00 to $18.00 per share.
bids.exact_quote: On July 24, 2013, Party B submitted a preliminary indication of interest at an all-cash purchase price of $17.00 to $18.00 per share.
bids.label: Party B preliminary bid

bids.description: Party C orally presented a preliminary all-cash indication at $15.00 to $17.00 per share.
bids.exact_quote: representatives from Party C called BofA Merrill Lynch and presented an oral preliminary indication of interest at an all-cash purchase price of $15.00 to $17.00 per share.
bids.label: Party C oral preliminary bid

bids.description: Party C submitted a written all-cash indication at $16.00 to $16.50 per share.
bids.exact_quote: Later on July 25, 2013, Party C submitted a written indication of interest at an all-cash purchase price of $16.00 to $16.50 per share.
bids.label: Party C written indication

bids.description: CSC/Pamplona submitted a revised all-cash indication at $19.50 per share, with Pamplona committed to provide 100% of capital and requesting two-week exclusivity.
bids.exact_quote: On September 9, 2013, CSC/Pamplona submitted a revised indication of interest with an all-cash purchase price of $19.50 per share, indicating that Pamplona was committed to provide 100% of the capital required to consummate the transaction and requesting that CSC/Pamplona and Mac-Gray agree to exclusive negotiations for two weeks.
bids.label: CSC/Pamplona revised bid

bids.description: Party B submitted a revised all-cash indication at $18.50 per share; proposed financing was affiliated-fund equity plus third-party debt, without a firm financing commitment.
bids.exact_quote: Party B also submitted a revised indication of interest with an all-cash purchase price of $18.50 per share and proposed to finance the transaction with a combination of equity from affiliated funds and third party debt. Party B's revised indication of interest did not include a firm financing commitment.
bids.label: Party B revised bid

bids.description: Party A submitted a revised all-cash indication at $18.00 to $19.00 per share, financed by outstanding credit facilities or affiliated equity sources, without a firm financing commitment.
bids.exact_quote: Party A submitted a revised indication of interest with an all-cash purchase price of $18.00 to $19.00 per share, to be financed by outstanding credit facilities or affiliated equity sources.
bids.label: Party A revised bid

bids.description: Party C orally revised its all-cash indication to $16.00 to $17.00 per share; no firm financing commitment was included.
bids.exact_quote: a representative of Party C indicated that it was submitting a revised oral indication of interest with an all-cash purchase price of $16.00 to $17.00 per share. Neither Party A's nor Party C's revised indication of interest included a firm financing commitment.
bids.label: Party C revised oral bid

bids.description: CSC/Pamplona submitted an all-cash $20.75 per share proposal, reiterated 100% Pamplona capital commitment, and requested two-week exclusivity.
bids.exact_quote: On September 18, 2013, CSC/Pamplona submitted a proposal for an all-cash purchase price of $20.75 per share, reiterating that Pamplona was committed to provide 100% of the capital required to consummate the transaction and requesting that CSC/Pamplona and Mac-Gray agree to engage in exclusive negotiations for two weeks.
bids.label: CSC/Pamplona September 18 bid

bids.description: Party A said its $18.00 to $19.00 per share all-cash proposal was its best and final offer.
bids.exact_quote: Party A reiterated in a telephone call to a representative of BofA Merrill Lynch that its previous indication of interest with an all-cash purchase price of $18.00 to $19.00 per share was its best and final offer.
bids.label: Party A best and final

bids.description: Party B submitted a proposal valued by Party B at $21.50 per share, consisting of $19.00 cash plus options valued by Party B at $2.50 per share.
bids.exact_quote: Party B also submitted a revised indication of interest with a purchase price that Party B valued at $21.50 per share, including $19.00 of cash to be paid at closing and the remaining per share price to be paid in the form of options to be issued to Mac-Gray stockholders, representing 10% of the new equity with a strike price equal to Party B's initial cost basis.
bids.label: Party B option proposal

bids.description: Party C dropped from the final indication stage by not submitting or reiterating an indication and giving no reason.
bids.exact_quote: Party C did not submit a revised indication of interest or reiterate its prior indication of interest of an all-cash purchase price of $16.00 to $17.00 per share nor did it specify any reasons in connection therewith.
bids.label: Party C no final bid

bids.description: CSC/Pamplona agreed to increase its all-cash price to $21.25 per share as last and best offer conditioned on two-week exclusivity.
bids.exact_quote: the representative of CSC/Pamplona called the members of the Transaction Committee to confirm that CSC/Pamplona were willing to increase their all-cash purchase price to $21.25 per share as a last and best offer, provided that Mac-Gray agree to a two week period of exclusive negotiations.
bids.label: CSC/Pamplona final price

bids.description: CSC/Pamplona requested voting agreements from Mr. MacDonald and his immediate family.
bids.exact_quote: The representative of CSC/Pamplona also requested that shares of Mac-Gray common stock owned beneficially and of record by Mr. MacDonald and his immediate family be subject to a customary voting agreement, which we refer to as the MacDonald voting agreements.
bids.label: MacDonald voting agreement request

chronology.description: Mac-Gray and CSC had earlier discussed a possible acquisition of CSC by Mac-Gray.
chronology.exact_quote: including discussions between Mac-Gray and CSC in the winter of 2011 and into 2012 regarding the possible acquisition of CSC by Mac-Gray.
chronology.label: 2011-2012 discussions

chronology.description: Mac-Gray engaged BofA Merrill Lynch for its consideration of a possible acquisition of CSC.
chronology.exact_quote: In connection with Mac-Gray's consideration of a possible acquisition of CSC, which we refer to as the 2011-2012 Discussions, Mac-Gray engaged BofA Merrill Lynch on October 23, 2012.
chronology.label: BofA prior engagement

chronology.description: Mac-Gray Board authorized management to work with BofA Merrill Lynch to explore transactional opportunities, including with Party A.
chronology.exact_quote: the Board discussed the sale of CSC to Pamplona as well as industry-wide merger and acquisition activity and dynamics, and authorized Mac-Gray management to work with BofA Merrill Lynch to explore transactional opportunities that might be available to Mac-Gray, including opportunities with another strategic party, whom we refer to as Party A
chronology.label: Board authorizes exploration

chronology.description: BofA Merrill Lynch contacted Party A, whose representative said Party A might consider a combination with Mac-Gray.
chronology.exact_quote: On April 8, 2013, representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch, as instructed by the Board, telephoned a representative of Party A to discuss generally a possible business combination between Party A and Mac-Gray.
chronology.label: Initial Party A contact

chronology.description: The Board reviewed strategic alternatives including existing business plan, stock repurchase, acquisitions, and a possible sale process.
chronology.exact_quote: BofA Merrill Lynch discussed CSC's sale process and the acquisition environment generally and provided an overview of strategic alternatives for Mac-Gray, including the continued pursuit of Mac-Gray's existing business plan, a significant stock repurchase program, acquisition opportunities and the possibility of a sale process.
chronology.label: Strategic alternatives review

chronology.description: The Board formed a Transaction Committee to help administer the strategic alternatives review and financial advisor selection.
chronology.exact_quote: To assist in the administration of the strategic alternatives review, the Board formed a transaction committee at this meeting, which we refer to as the Transaction Committee
chronology.label: Transaction Committee formed

chronology.description: The Transaction Committee decided to invite three nationally recognized investment banks to submit financial advisor proposals.
chronology.exact_quote: The Transaction Committee discussed the process for hiring a financial advisor for a review of strategic alternatives and determined to invite three nationally recognized investment banks to submit proposals.
chronology.label: Advisor RFP

chronology.description: Mac-Gray terminated BofA Merrill Lynch's prior engagement for the 2011-2012 CSC acquisition discussions.
chronology.exact_quote: On May 15, 2013, Mac-Gray sent a letter to BofA Merrill Lynch terminating their engagement in connection with the 2011-2012 Discussions.
chronology.label: Prior BofA engagement terminated

chronology.description: The Board approved engaging BofA Merrill Lynch for exploration of strategic alternatives, including a potential sale process.
chronology.exact_quote: The Transaction Committee recommended to the Board that Mac-Gray engage BofA Merrill Lynch as Mac-Gray's financial advisor for the Board's exploration of strategic alternatives, including a potential sale process.
chronology.label: BofA selected

chronology.description: Mac-Gray entered into BofA engagement letter, with BofA agreeing not to provide new acquisition financing to potential buyers without Mac-Gray consent.
chronology.exact_quote: Mac-Gray entered into an engagement letter with BofA Merrill Lynch with respect to the Board's undertaking a review of strategic alternatives, including a potential sale process, and in which BofA Merrill Lynch agreed not to engage as a possible source of new acquisition financing for any potential buyers in connection with any such sale process without Mac-Gray's prior written consent.
chronology.label: BofA engagement letter

chronology.description: Party A submitted its unsolicited all-cash proposal.
chronology.exact_quote: On June 21, 2013, Party A submitted an unsolicited proposal to the Mac-Gray Board of Directors
chronology.label: Party A unsolicited proposal

chronology.description: Board decided to exclude Mr. MacDonald and Mr. Rothenberg from evaluation of strategic alternatives and negotiations with third parties.
chronology.exact_quote: the Board decided, and Mr. MacDonald and Mr. Rothenberg agreed, that it would be appropriate to exclude Mr. MacDonald and Mr. Rothenberg from the Board's evaluation of strategic alternatives and any negotiations with third parties that might take place.
chronology.label: Recusals/conflict management

chronology.description: The Special Committee was established with authority to evaluate proposals, negotiate material terms, consider alternatives, and recommend action to the Board.
chronology.exact_quote: the Board then established the Special Committee, comprised of the six independent and disinterested members of the Board...to, among other things, explore strategic alternatives to enhance value to Mac-Gray's stockholders and to recommend to the Board the advisability of any such strategic alternative.
chronology.label: Special Committee established

chronology.description: The Special Committee concluded it was in stockholders' best interests to consider a potential sale of Mac-Gray versus standalone alternatives.
chronology.exact_quote: the Special Committee concluded that it would be in the best interests of stockholders to consider a potential sale of Mac-Gray and to review this alternative as compared to Mac-Gray's continuing as a stand-alone company
chronology.label: Sale process decision

chronology.description: The Special Committee determined to approach 50 parties total: 15 strategic and 35 financial buyers.
chronology.exact_quote: the Special Committee determined to approach a total of 50 parties, including CSC and Pamplona, who together we refer to as CSC/Pamplona, and Party A, to solicit potential interest in a transaction with Mac-Gray, 15 of which were potential strategic buyers and 35 of which were potential financial buyers, including private equity firms.
chronology.label: Auction outreach plan

chronology.description: BofA contacted strategic and financial parties identified by the Special Committee.
chronology.exact_quote: During the next several weeks, in accordance with the directives of the Special Committee, BofA Merrill Lynch contacted the strategic and financial parties identified at the June 24th Special Committee meeting.
chronology.label: Bidder outreach

chronology.date: Over next two months after June 24, 2013
chronology.description: Twenty potential bidders entered confidentiality agreements: two strategic bidders and eighteen financial bidders.
chronology.exact_quote: Over the next two months a total of 20 potential bidders, including two strategic bidders (Party A and CSC/Pamplona) and 18 financial bidders (including Party B and Party C), entered into confidentiality agreements with Mac-Gray
chronology.label: NDA participation

chronology.description: Special Committee denied CSC/Pamplona's request that Moab be allowed to partner exclusively with CSC/Pamplona at that stage.
chronology.exact_quote: Because the objective of the sale process was to maximize value for Mac-Gray's stockholders, the Special Committee decided to deny the exclusivity request at this stage of the process so that the potential equity rollover of Moab's shares could be available as a source of capital for other potential bidders.
chronology.label: Moab exclusivity denied

chronology.description: Special Committee reviewed first-round indications from Party B, Party C, CSC/Pamplona and Party A's June proposal; Mr. Rothenberg recused himself.
chronology.exact_quote: representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch reviewed the preliminary indications of interest received from Party B, Party C and CSC/Pamplona as well as the Party A June 21 proposal
chronology.label: First-round bid review

chronology.description: The Special Committee authorized staged disclosure and management meetings with Party A, Party B, Party C and CSC/Pamplona.
chronology.exact_quote: the Special Committee authorized and instructed BofA Merrill Lynch to proceed with the staged disclosure as discussed at this meeting and to reach out to each of the four interested bidders and arrange management meetings.
chronology.label: Second-stage process authorized

chronology.description: Management presentations were held with Party B, Party C, CSC/Pamplona, and Party A.
chronology.exact_quote: On August 6, 2013, representatives of Party B attended a management presentation... On August 8, 2013, representatives of Party C attended a management presentation... On August 12, 2013, representatives of CSC and Pamplona attended a management presentation... On August 14, 2013, representatives of Party A attended a management presentation
chronology.label: Management presentations

chronology.description: Four bidders conducted business and legal diligence; financial bidders received broader data-room access than strategic bidders.
chronology.exact_quote: Between August 6, 2013 and August 27, 2013, the four bidders conducted business and legal due diligence. During this time, Party B and Party C, in light of the fact that they were financial bidders, were given broad access to an electronic data site, and Party A and CSC/Pamplona, in light of the fact that they were strategic bidders, were given somewhat limited access to this electronic data site
chronology.label: Diligence/data-room staging

chronology.description: BofA sent letters to Party A, Party B, Party C and CSC/Pamplona requesting revised written proposals by September 9.
chronology.exact_quote: BofA Merrill Lynch, as instructed by the Special Committee, sent a letter to each of Party A, Party B, Party C and CSC/Pamplona instructing such bidders to submit revised written proposals no later than September 9, 2013
chronology.label: Revised bid request

chronology.description: Mr. MacDonald said he was not interested in rolling over any shares.
chronology.exact_quote: During this call, Mr. MacDonald indicated that he had decided that he was not interested in rolling over any of his shares in any transaction.
chronology.label: MacDonald no rollover

chronology.description: Special Committee instructed BofA to request final indications of interest from all four bidders by September 18, 2013.
chronology.exact_quote: the Special Committee instructed BofA Merrill Lynch to call each of Party A, Party B, Party C and CSC/Pamplona to request final indications of interest by September 18, 2013
chronology.label: Final bid request

chronology.description: Party C did not submit or reiterate a final indication of interest.
chronology.exact_quote: Party C did not submit a revised indication of interest or reiterate its prior indication of interest
chronology.label: Party C drops

chronology.description: Special Committee favored CSC/Pamplona over Party B despite Party B's higher face value, citing option complexity and financing risk.
chronology.exact_quote: the Special Committee concluded that the revised indication of interest submitted by CSC/Pamplona had the potential of delivering the most value to stockholders, provided greater certainty and involved less potential risk than the other indications.
chronology.label: CSC/Pamplona preferred

chronology.description: Special Committee authorized entry into exclusivity with CSC/Pamplona until October 12 on final proposal terms.
chronology.exact_quote: the Special Committee authorized Mac-Gray to enter into an exclusivity agreement providing for exclusive negotiations until 5 p.m. ET on October 12, 2013 on the terms included in the CSC/Pamplona final proposal
chronology.label: Exclusivity authorized

chronology.description: CSC, Pamplona and Mac-Gray executed exclusivity agreement through October 12, subject to conditions.
chronology.exact_quote: On September 24, 2013, CSC, Pamplona and Mac-Gray executed the exclusivity agreement providing for exclusive negotiations until 5 p.m. ET on October 12, 2013, subject to certain conditions
chronology.label: Exclusivity executed

chronology.description: Mac-Gray and CSC/Pamplona negotiated merger agreement and Pamplona commitment letter; CSC/Pamplona received full data-room access and conducted confirmatory diligence.
chronology.exact_quote: Between September 25, 2013 and October 7, 2013, the Transaction Committee and representatives from CSC/Pamplona, Goodwin Procter and Kirkland negotiated the terms of the merger agreement... Also during this time, CSC and Pamplona were granted full access to the electronic data site and access to certain members of management and conducted confirmatory business and legal due diligence on Mac-Gray.
chronology.label: Definitive agreement negotiations

chronology.description: Mr. MacDonald, his wife and one of his trusts entered voting agreements effective upon merger agreement execution.
chronology.exact_quote: the terms of the MacDonald voting agreements, which were entered into by Mr. MacDonald, his wife and one of his trusts on September 27, 2013, and which became effective upon entry into the merger agreement.
chronology.label: MacDonald voting agreements signed

chronology.description: Moab decided not to roll over any shares in the transaction after discussions with Pamplona.
chronology.exact_quote: after discussions between Moab and representatives of Pamplona, it was determined that Moab would not rollover any Moab shares in the transaction.
chronology.label: Moab no rollover

chronology.description: CSC/Pamplona agreed to an $11 million Mac-Gray termination fee.
chronology.exact_quote: the representative of CSC/Pamplona telephoned Mr. Daoust and agreed to the $11 million Mac-Gray termination fee.
chronology.label: Termination fee agreement

chronology.description: CSC, Pamplona and Mac-Gray extended exclusivity to October 15 at 11:59 p.m. ET.
chronology.exact_quote: On October 12, 2013, CSC, Pamplona and Mac-Gray executed a letter agreement extending exclusivity until 11:59 p.m. ET on October 15, 2013.
chronology.label: Exclusivity extension

chronology.description: Special Committee unanimously recommended the merger agreement and merger to the Board after receiving BofA's fairness opinion.
chronology.exact_quote: the Special Committee unanimously determined to recommend to the Board that the merger agreement and the merger were advisable and in the best interests of Mac-Gray's stockholders
chronology.label: Special Committee approval

chronology.description: Full Board unanimously approved the merger agreement and recommended stockholder approval.
chronology.exact_quote: the Board (upon the recommendation of the Special Committee) unanimously determined that the merger agreement and the merger were advisable and in the best interests of Mac-Gray's stockholders, approved the merger on the terms and subject to the conditions set forth in the merger agreement, adopted the merger agreement and recommended that the Mac-Gray stockholders adopt and approve the merger agreement.
chronology.label: Board approval

chronology.description: Merger agreement executed; Pamplona commitment letter delivered; Moab voting agreement executed; MacDonald voting agreements became effective.
chronology.exact_quote: Later in the day, on October 14, 2013, the merger agreement was executed, the Pamplona commitment letter was delivered, Moab and Parent executed the Moab voting agreement and the Mac-Donald voting agreements became effective.
chronology.label: Signing

chronology.description: Mac-Gray and CSC announced the executed merger agreement before markets opened.
chronology.exact_quote: On October 15, 2013, before the U.S. stock markets opened, Mac-Gray and CSC jointly announced the execution of the merger agreement.
chronology.label: Announcement

counts.description: Special Committee decided to approach 50 parties in total.
counts.exact_quote: the Special Committee determined to approach a total of 50 parties
counts.label: 50 parties approached

counts.description: The 50-party outreach consisted of 15 potential strategic buyers and 35 potential financial buyers.
counts.exact_quote: 15 of which were potential strategic buyers and 35 of which were potential financial buyers, including private equity firms.
counts.label: 15 strategic / 35 financial

counts.date: Over next two months after June 24, 2013
counts.description: Twenty potential bidders entered confidentiality agreements.
counts.exact_quote: Over the next two months a total of 20 potential bidders...entered into confidentiality agreements with Mac-Gray
counts.label: 20 NDAs

counts.date: Over next two months after June 24, 2013
counts.description: The 20 NDA parties included 2 strategic bidders and 18 financial bidders.
counts.exact_quote: including two strategic bidders (Party A and CSC/Pamplona) and 18 financial bidders (including Party B and Party C)
counts.label: 2 strategic / 18 financial bidders

counts.description: Special Committee had six independent and disinterested members.
counts.exact_quote: the Board then established the Special Committee, comprised of the six independent and disinterested members of the Board
counts.label: 6 Special Committee members

counts.description: Transaction Committee invited three investment banks to submit proposals for the financial advisor role.
counts.exact_quote: determined to invite three nationally recognized investment banks to submit proposals.
counts.label: 3 advisor candidates

counts.description: Exclusivity with CSC/Pamplona was to last until 5 p.m. ET on October 12, 2013.
counts.exact_quote: exclusive negotiations until 5 p.m. ET on October 12, 2013
counts.label: Exclusivity deadline

counts.description: CSC reverse termination fee negotiated at $15 million for failure to obtain regulatory clearance.
counts.exact_quote: the inclusion of a $15 million "reverse" termination fee payable by CSC/Pamplona to Mac-Gray in the event regulatory clearance is not obtained
counts.label: $15 million reverse termination fee

counts.description: Mac-Gray termination fee agreed at $11 million, approximately 3.2% of total equity value.
counts.exact_quote: a Mac-Gray termination fee of not more than $11 million, which represented approximately 3.2% of the total equity value of the transaction.
counts.label: $11 million termination fee

counts.description: Pamplona liability cap for certain damages was agreed at $50 million.
counts.exact_quote: The Transaction Committee reported that the parties had come to an agreement that Pamplona's liability for certain damages would be limited to $50 million
counts.label: $50 million Pamplona liability cap

counts.description: BofA Merrill Lynch's merger advisory fee was estimated at approximately $8.1 million, with $0.75 million payable upon opinion delivery and the balance contingent on closing.
counts.exact_quote: Mac-Gray has agreed to pay BofA Merrill Lynch for its services in connection with the merger an aggregate fee currently estimated to be approximately $8.1 million, $0.75 million of which was payable upon delivery of its opinion and the balance of which is contingent upon the completion of the merger.
counts.label: BofA fee

relations.description: Mr. MacDonald and Mr. Rothenberg were excluded from strategic alternative evaluation and negotiations due to potential conflicts tied to equity stakes/rollover possibilities.
relations.exact_quote: it would be appropriate to exclude Mr. MacDonald and Mr. Rothenberg from the Board's evaluation of strategic alternatives and any negotiations with third parties that might take place.
relations.label: Conflict recusals

relations.description: The Transaction Committee identified possible misalignment for Mr. MacDonald because of family stock ownership and potential third-party partnering or rollover.
relations.exact_quote: in view of the significant stock ownership of Mr. MacDonald and his family, coupled with the possibility that the strategic alternatives process might result in third parties who would be interested in partnering with Mr. MacDonald (either through continued employment and/or contribution of some or all of his existing equity, a so-called equity "rollover"), Mr. MacDonald could have interests that would not be aligned with other Mac-Gray stockholders.
relations.label: MacDonald potential conflict

relations.description: Mr. Rothenberg would need to be excluded if Moab were interested in equity rollover.
relations.exact_quote: to the extent Moab might have an interest in an equity rollover of all or a portion of Moab's shares of Mac-Gray common stock in connection with any sale transaction that might result from the Board's strategic alternatives process, Mr. Rothenberg would need to be excluded from the process from the outset.
relations.label: Moab/Rothenberg potential conflict

relations.description: Pamplona wanted its confidentiality agreement to allow bid/evaluation discussions with Moab and exclusive engagement by Moab with CSC/Pamplona.
relations.exact_quote: Pamplona would be requesting that any confidentiality agreement CSC/Pamplona execute with Mac-Gray permit CSC and Pamplona to discuss their bid and evaluation material with Moab, with Moab agreeing to exclusively engage with CSC/Pamplona.
relations.label: Pamplona-Moab exclusivity request

relations.description: Special Committee denied Moab's exclusive partnership with CSC/Pamplona at that stage to preserve Moab rollover as potential capital for other bidders.
relations.exact_quote: the Special Committee decided to deny the exclusivity request at this stage of the process so that the potential equity rollover of Moab's shares could be available as a source of capital for other potential bidders.
relations.label: Moab rollover availability

relations.description: BofA told the Special Committee bidders were unlikely to proceed without Mr. MacDonald's express support.
relations.exact_quote: BofA Merrill Lynch then indicated that, based on conversations with Party A, Party B, Party C and CSC/Pamplona, it was unlikely that any of these parties would move forward in the process without the express support of Mr. MacDonald.
relations.label: Need for MacDonald support

relations.description: Mr. MacDonald generally supported continuing the process but wanted higher value.
relations.exact_quote: Although Mr. MacDonald voiced his general support for continuing with the process, he expressed his view that Mac-Gray should continue to seek a higher value for stockholders.
relations.label: MacDonald support / higher price

relations.description: Special Committee viewed Party B financing as riskier than Pamplona-funded CSC/Pamplona proposal with no third-party financing required.
relations.exact_quote: Party B would likely be financing the transaction with a combination of equity from affiliated funds and third party debt capital, which would involve more risk than the Pamplona-funded CSC/Pamplona proposal, which did not require any third party financing.
relations.label: Financing certainty comparison

relations.description: Final CSC/Pamplona proposal included no financing contingency and Pamplona funding 100% of required capital.
relations.exact_quote: the fact that there would be no financing contingency and that Pamplona was committing to fund 100% of the capital needed for closing, which, together with the all-cash per share purchase price of $21.25, we refer to as the CSC/Pamplona final proposal.
relations.label: Pamplona funding commitment

relations.description: MacDonald voting agreements were negotiated and entered by Mr. MacDonald, his wife and one trust, effective at merger agreement execution.
relations.exact_quote: Kirkland and outside counsel to Mr. MacDonald negotiated the terms of the MacDonald voting agreements, which were entered into by Mr. MacDonald, his wife and one of his trusts on September 27, 2013, and which became effective upon entry into the merger agreement.
relations.label: MacDonald voting agreements

relations.description: Moab discussed possible share rollover with Pamplona but ultimately did not roll over any shares.
relations.exact_quote: Mr. Rothenberg engaged in various discussions with representatives of Pamplona during this time to discuss a possible rollover of Moab shares. As a result of such discussions, it was determined that Moab would not rollover any Moab shares in the transaction.
relations.label: Moab rollover discussions/no rollover

relations.description: Moab negotiated a voting agreement with CSC/Pamplona's counsel and later executed it with Parent.
relations.exact_quote: Between October 8, 2013 and October 14, 2013, Kirkland and counsel for Moab negotiated the terms of a voting agreement for Moab.
relations.label: Moab voting agreement

relations.description: BofA fairness opinion was delivered to the Special Committee for its evaluation of merger consideration from a financial point of view and was not a stockholder voting recommendation.
relations.exact_quote: BofA Merrill Lynch delivered its opinion to the Special Committee for the benefit and use of the Special Committee (in its capacity as such) in connection with and for purposes of its evaluation of the merger consideration from a financial point of view.
relations.label: BofA opinion relationship

source_limitations: The provided window covers background of the merger and selected financial-advisor/projection sections, but does not include the full merger agreement terms beginning on page 60 or tender-offer mechanics; the transaction appears presented as a stockholder vote merger, not a tender offer, in this excerpt.
Several bidders are anonymized as Party A, Party B and Party C; the source does not disclose their identities in this window.

The window states that 50 parties were approached and 20 entered confidentiality agreements, but does not provide identities for the other contacted parties or NDA parties beyond CSC/Pamplona, Party A, Party B and Party C.
The provided text includes final execution and announcement dates but not closing/completion date or regulatory approval outcome.
The excerpt includes only some sections of the reasons-for-merger pages; page placeholders indicate factors but not the detailed bullet text in this window.

uncertain_or_missing.description: Party A's identity is not disclosed in the excerpt.
uncertain_or_missing.exact_quote: another strategic party, whom we refer to as Party A
uncertain_or_missing.label: Party A identity missing

uncertain_or_missing.description: Party B and Party C identities are not disclosed in the excerpt.
uncertain_or_missing.exact_quote: 18 financial bidders (including Party B and Party C)
uncertain_or_missing.label: Party B/C identities missing

uncertain_or_missing.description: Party C gave no reason for not submitting or reiterating a final bid.
uncertain_or_missing.exact_quote: Party C did not submit a revised indication of interest or reiterate its prior indication of interest of an all-cash purchase price of $16.00 to $17.00 per share nor did it specify any reasons in connection therewith.
uncertain_or_missing.label: Party C withdrawal reason missing

uncertain_or_missing.description: Party A, Party B and Party C did not provide firm financing commitments in revised indications; CSC/Pamplona did provide 100% capital commitment.
uncertain_or_missing.exact_quote: Party B's revised indication of interest did not include a firm financing commitment... Neither Party A's nor Party C's revised indication of interest included a firm financing commitment.
uncertain_or_missing.label: No firm financing for competing bids

uncertain_or_missing.description: Party A delayed entering an NDA because counsel did not initially agree on customer and employee non-solicitation provisions.
uncertain_or_missing.exact_quote: outside counsel to Party A and representatives of Goodwin Procter negotiated the terms of a confidentiality and standstill agreement, but did not come to agreement on the terms of the customer and employee non-solicitation provisions.
uncertain_or_missing.label: Party A NDA delay

uncertain_or_missing.description: The actual incremental value of Party B options was uncertain and viewed as risky/complex by the Special Committee, despite Party B's asserted $2.50 per share value.
uncertain_or_missing.exact_quote: The Special Committee discussed the risks and complexities of the Party B options, including their contingent and deferred nature and the uncertainties associated with how Mac-Gray's performance would be measured
uncertain_or_missing.label: Party B option value uncertainty

uncertain_or_missing.description: No tender offer evidence appears in this source window; the transaction is described as requiring stockholder adoption of a merger agreement.
uncertain_or_missing.exact_quote: Under applicable law, we cannot complete the merger without the vote of a majority of the outstanding shares of Mac-Gray common stock entitled to vote at the special meeting voting in favor of the proposal to adopt the merger agreement.
uncertain_or_missing.label: No tender-offer process shown
